paragraphs.en: Lucy and her mum go to a clothes shop in town. Lucy needs a new shirt for school and a dress for her cousin's birthday party. The shop is big and there are many nice clothes.
paragraphs.fr: Lucy et sa maman vont dans un magasin de vetements en ville. Lucy a besoin d'une nouvelle chemise pour l'ecole et d'une robe pour l'anniversaire de sa cousine. Le magasin est grand et il y a beaucoup de beaux vetements.

paragraphs.en: Yes, please. I'm looking for a shirt. Do you have this shirt in a small size?
paragraphs.fr: Oui, s'il vous plait. Je cherche une chemise. Avez-vous cette chemise en petite taille ?
paragraphs.speaker: Lucy

paragraphs.en: Let me check. Yes, we have it in small and medium. What colour do you want?
paragraphs.fr: Laissez-moi verifier. Oui, nous l'avons en petit et en moyen. Quelle couleur voulez-vous ?
paragraphs.speaker: Shop assistant

paragraphs.en: I like the blue one. Can I try this on, please?
paragraphs.fr: J'aime la bleue. Est-ce que je peux l'essayer, s'il vous plait ?
paragraphs.speaker: Lucy

paragraphs.en: Of course! The changing rooms are over there.
paragraphs.fr: Bien sur ! Les cabines d'essayage sont la-bas.
paragraphs.speaker: Shop assistant

paragraphs.en: Lucy tries on the blue shirt. She comes back to show her mum.
paragraphs.fr: Lucy essaie la chemise bleue. Elle revient pour la montrer a sa maman.

paragraphs.en: How is it, darling? Does it fit?
paragraphs.fr: Comment c'est, ma cherie ? Ca te va ?
paragraphs.speaker: Mum

paragraphs.en: It's too big, Mum. Do you have it in a smaller size?
paragraphs.fr: C'est trop grand, Maman. L'avez-vous dans une taille plus petite ?
paragraphs.speaker: Lucy

paragraphs.en: I'm sorry, we don't have a smaller size in blue. But we have this one in white. It's the same style.
paragraphs.fr: Je suis desolee, nous n'avons pas de taille plus petite en bleu. Mais nous avons celle-ci en blanc. C'est le meme style.
paragraphs.speaker: Shop assistant

paragraphs.en: OK, I'll take the white one. And I also need a dress. I like that red dress over there. How much is it?
paragraphs.fr: D'accord, je prends la blanche. Et j'ai aussi besoin d'une robe. J'aime cette robe rouge la-bas. Combien coute-t-elle ?
paragraphs.speaker: Lucy

paragraphs.en: That dress is twenty-five pounds. Would you like to try it on?
paragraphs.fr: Cette robe coute vingt-cinq livres. Voulez-vous l'essayer ?
paragraphs.speaker: Shop assistant

paragraphs.en: Yes, please! Lucy tries on the red dress. It fits perfectly. She is very happy with her new clothes.
paragraphs.fr: Oui, s'il vous plait ! Lucy essaie la robe rouge. Elle lui va parfaitement. Elle est tres contente de ses nouveaux vetements.

vocabulary.definition_en: un vetement pour le haut du corps avec des manches et des boutons
vocabulary.example: He wears a white shirt to school every day.

vocabulary.definition_en: un vetement qui couvre chaque jambe separement
vocabulary.example: I need a new pair of trousers for work.

vocabulary.definition_en: un vetement pour les femmes ou les filles qui couvre le corps et une partie des jambes
vocabulary.example: She is wearing a beautiful red dress.

vocabulary.definition_en: a quel point quelque chose est grand ou petit
vocabulary.example: What size do you wear? — I wear a medium.
This shirt is too small for me. I need a medium.

vocabulary.definition_en: entre petit et grand en taille
vocabulary.example: I usually wear a medium size.
Do you have this in large? The medium is too small.

vocabulary.definition_en: mettre un vetement pour voir s'il va bien
vocabulary.example: Can I try on these trousers, please?

grammar_points.title: 'Can I try this on?' — Polite Requests with 'Can I...?'
grammar_points.explanation: On utilise **'Can I...?'** pour faire des demandes polies dans les magasins et les situations quotidiennes. Apres 'Can I', on utilise la base verbale. **'Try on'** est un verbe a particule — quand on utilise un pronom, il se place au milieu : 'try **it** on' (pas 'try on it').
examples: Can I try this on? (= Est-ce que je peux essayer ceci ?)
Can I try it on? (pronoun goes in the middle)
Can I have a bag, please?

grammar_points.title: Demonstratives: this / that / these / those
grammar_points.explanation: On utilise **this** (singulier, proche) et **these** (pluriel, proche) pour les choses pres de nous. On utilise **that** (singulier, loin) et **those** (pluriel, loin) pour les choses plus eloignees.
examples: I like this shirt. (= the shirt I am holding / near me)
I like that dress over there. (= the dress far from me)
These shoes are nice. (= shoes near me, plural)
Those trousers are expensive. (= trousers far from me, plural)

expressions.meaning_fr: Est-ce que je peux essayer ceci ?
expressions.example: I like this jacket. Can I try it on?
expressions.note: L'expression essentielle pour faire du shopping de vetements. Retenez : 'try it on' (pas 'try on it').

expressions.example: This shirt is too big. Do you have a smaller one?
expressions.note: 'Too' signifie plus que ce qui est souhaitable. Il a un sens negatif.

expressions.example: I like this dress. Do you have it in blue?
expressions.note: S'utilise pour demander une taille ou une couleur differente : 'Do you have it in medium?' ou 'Do you have it in red?'

written.question: Why does Lucy want a new dress?
written.explanation: The text says 'Lucy needs a dress for her cousin's birthday party.'

written.question: What is the problem with the blue shirt?
Complete: 'I like _____ dress over there.'
written.explanation: 'Over there' means the dress is far away, so we use 'that' (singular, far).

written.sentence: Can I try ________ on, please? (this shirt)
written.hint: When we use a pronoun with 'try on', the pronoun goes in the middle: try __ on.

written.sentence: This shirt is too ________. Do you have it in a ________ size?
written.hint: Lucy's shirt doesn't fit because it is not the right size.

written.french: Est-ce que je peux essayer cette robe ? — Bien sur ! Les cabines d'essayage sont la-bas.
written.suggested_answer: Can I try this dress on? — Of course! The changing rooms are over there.

oral.instruction: Read this shopping dialogue aloud. Use a polite, friendly tone.
oral.text:
Lucy: Can I try this on, please?
Shop assistant: Of course! The changing rooms are over there.
Lucy: It's too big. Do you have it in a smaller size?
Shop assistant: I'm sorry, we don't have a smaller size in blue.
oral.focus: Use rising intonation for questions and a polite, friendly tone throughout. Practise 'Can I try this on?'

oral.question: You are in a clothes shop. Ask for a shirt in your size, try it on, and say if it fits or not.
oral.suggested_answer: Hello, I'm looking for a shirt. Do you have this shirt in a medium? Can I try it on, please? It fits perfectly! How much is it?

oral.question: What clothes are you wearing today? Describe them using colours and sizes.
oral.suggested_answer: Today I am wearing a blue shirt and black trousers. My shirt is a medium size. I am also wearing white shoes. I like comfortable clothes.
